programme.title: Welcome to the Punch
programme.desc: When a notorious criminal is forced to return to London, it gives a veteran detective one final chance to bring the man to justice.

programme.title: A Score to Settle
programme.desc: Diagnosed with a fatal condition, a mob enforcer leaves prison after 19 years and plots a bloody course of revenge against his old gang.

programme.title: No Man's Land
programme.desc: When a vigilante border patrol turns fatal, a man flees on horseback to Mexico, seeking forgiveness from the victim's father.

programme.title: The Postcard Killings
programme.desc: A New York detective investigates the death of his daughter, who was murdered while on her honeymoon in London, the victim of an apparent serial killer.

programme.title: Nitram
programme.desc: Living a life of isolation and frustration, a young man develops an unexpected friendship with a reclusive heiress. When that relationship meets its tragic end, his loneliness and anger culminates into the most nihilistic and heinous of acts.

programme.title: A Lonely Place to Die
programme.desc: Five mountaineers stumble into a vicious kidnapping scheme after they rescue a child they find buried alive in the Scottish Highlands.

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Venice
programme.desc: To get his dog back, private eye Steve Ford forges an unlikely alliance with Spyder, the leader of a local gang. With help from his best friend, Steve pulls out the big guns to retrieve Spyder's missing cash and cocaine and save his beloved pooch.

programme.title: A Score to Settle
programme.desc: Diagnosed with a fatal condition, a mob enforcer leaves prison after 19 years and plots a bloody course of revenge against his old gang.

programme.title: Re: Born
programme.desc: A legendary soldier with a mysterious past decides to unleash his inner beast to stand up for what he cares about.

programme.title: True History of the Kelly Gang
programme.desc: An exploration of Australian bushranger Ned Kelly and his gang as they attempt to evade authorities during the 1870s.

programme.title: Exam
programme.desc: Eight candidates cooperate in order to secure a job with a prestigious company.

programme.title: The Tax Collector
programme.desc: Two enforcers for a crime lord face an uncertain future when an old rival reappears.

programme.title: Dog Eat Dog
programme.desc: Three ex-cons (Nicolas Cage, Willem Dafoe, Christopher Matthew Cook) find themselves on the run from the mob and the police after botching a plan to kidnap a baby for a large ransom.

programme.title: Welcome to the Punch
programme.desc: When a notorious criminal is forced to return to London, it gives a veteran detective one final chance to bring the man to justice.

programme.title: A Lonely Place to Die
programme.desc: Five mountaineers stumble into a vicious kidnapping scheme after they rescue a child they find buried alive in the Scottish Highlands.

programme.title: American Fable
programme.desc: A young girl (Peyton Kennedy) learns that her beloved father is hiding a wealthy man in a silo to save their struggling farm.

programme.title: Backcountry
programme.desc: A couple (Missy Peregrym, Jeff Roop) on a deep-wilderness hike become hopelessly lost within an aggressive black bear's territory.

programme.title: Baskin
programme.desc: Policemen (Gorkem Kasal, Ergun Kuyucu) face a night of horror in a subterranean labyrinth after answering a distress call.

programme.title: Centigrade
programme.desc: A man and his pregnant wife become trapped in their car when a storm buries them underneath layers of snow and ice. As supplies dwindle and temperatures plunge, the couple must battle the elements and hypothermia in a desperate fight for survival.

programme.title: Desolation
programme.desc: When a widow ventures into a remote wilderness area with her son and best friend to spread her husband's ashes, a mysterious hiker begins dogging them and forces them to confront their deepest fears.

programme.title: Devil's Gate
programme.desc: An FBI agent aims to solve a missing persons case in a rural area, but when she discovers that mysterious creatures may be responsible for the crime, she must tap into every reserve of her courage and smarts to survive.

programme.title: Entrance
programme.desc: Things lurking on the periphery of people's lives lead to horrific conclusions.

programme.title: Extraterrestrial
programme.desc: Malevolent aliens interrupt five collegians' vacation at a remote woodland cabin.

programme.title: The Hunt
programme.desc: While making an instructional video, hunters become the prey after they trespass on government land.

programme.title: I Trapped the Devil
programme.desc: Hoping for a joyful family reunion, Matt and his wife pay a surprise visit to the home of his estranged brother, Steve, to celebrate Christmas. To their shock, they soon learn that Steve has a hostage in his basement -- a man he claims is the devil.

programme.title: Into the Ashes
programme.desc: Nick is an ex-con who's adjusting to a new job, a new wife and some semblance of normalcy in rural Alabama. When vengeful former associates track him down, he's soon forced back into the desperate and violent life that he thought he left behind.

programme.title: Surveillance
programme.desc: FBI agents (Julia Ormond, Bill Pullman) interrogate witnesses who survive the bloody rampage of two serial killers.

programme.title: The Numbers Station
programme.desc: Emerson, a disgraced CIA agent, guards Katherine, a code operator, in a top secret CIA installation in Britain. When heavily armed assailants lay siege to the compound, Emerson and Katherine have to stop the deadly plot before it's too late.

programme.title: Muzzle
programme.desc: A Los Angeles police officer and his K-9 partner try to uncover a vast conspiracy that has a chokehold on the city.

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Venice
programme.desc: To get his dog back, private eye Steve Ford forges an unlikely alliance with Spyder, the leader of a local gang. With help from his best friend, Steve pulls out the big guns to retrieve Spyder's missing cash and cocaine and save his beloved pooch.

programme.title: Lake of Death
programme.desc: Lillian and some friends travel back to the remote family cabin where her twin brother died the previous year. Soon, strange things start happening.

programme.title: Official Secrets
programme.desc: A British intelligence officer turns into a whistleblower after learning about NSA espionage plans designed to ensure passage of a resolution of war against Iraq by the U.N. Security Council in 2003.

programme.title: Rage
programme.desc: Following the kidnapping and murder of his daughter (Aubrey Peeples), a reformed criminal (Nicolas Cage) returns to his old ways to exact vengeance.

programme.title: Prisoner X
programme.desc: A CIA agent arrives at a secret underground prison to interrogate a time-traveling terrorist believed to be behind a global civil war.

programme.title: Trench 11
programme.desc: In the final days of World War I, a soldier leads a team beneath the trenches to stop a German plot that could turn the tide of the entire war.

programme.title: Blood Glacier
programme.desc: Scientists discover that a red liquid leaking from a nearby glacier contains organisms that turn living hosts into deadly mutants.

programme.title: A Score to Settle

programme.title: She-Wolves: England's Early Queens
programme.desc: In the medieval and Tudor world there was no question in people's minds about the order of God's creation - men ruled and women didn't. A king was a warrior who literally fought to win power then battled to keep it. Yet despite everything that stood in their way, a handful of extraordinary women did attempt to rule medieval and Tudor England. In this series, historian Dr Helen Castor explores seven queens who challenged male power, the fierce reactions they provoked and whether the term 'she wolves' was deserved. Eight hundred years ago, Matilda came within a hair's breadth of being the first woman to be crowned queen of England in her own right. Castor explores how Matilda reached this point and why her bid for the throne ultimately failed. Her daughter-in-law Eleanor of Aquitaine was an equally formidable woman. Despite being remembered as the queen of courtly love, in reality during her long life she divorced one king and married another, only to lead a rebellion against him. She only finally achieved the power she craved in her seventies.

programme.title: Walking With...
programme.desc: Broadcaster Kate Garraway goes for an invigorating walk along the edge of the Cotswold Hills. As she passes through the patch where Kate first cut her teeth as a reporter, she takes time to appreciate the stunning landscapes. From the panoramic viewpoint of Painswick Beacon, Kate descends through beech woods to the village of Upton St Leonards, soaking up the sounds of nature as she goes. From there she climbs Cooper’s Hill and wanders down to the church at Great Witcombe. Kate relishes the chance to have nothing to do but take in the 'joy and rhythm of the countryside' as she crosses the gentle, rolling hills with just a 360-degree camera for company. Along the way, she meets local craftspeople who are inspired by the ‘living theatre’ of the landscapes. Kate finds her natural surroundings a source of inspiration and resilience as she reflects on a year of challenges and change.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: At the home of Scottish football, Glasgow’s Hampden Park stadium, Michael admires the oldest football trophy in the world and hears how the 'passing game' was born there. From Scotland’s busiest railway station, the splendid Glasgow Central, Michael heads to the M8 motorway to see 16 lanes of traffic slicing through the city centre. At the Kelvingrove Art Gallery, Michael hears how, after the Second World War, artist Joan Eardley set up a studio amid the tenements of Townhead and painted children from Glasgow’s slums. Michael finishes this leg of the journey in the planned, modernist new town of Cumbernauld, where he hears about the utopian ideals that shaped its design and architecture.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his postwar Scottish railway adventure, this time from Glasgow to the capital, Edinburgh. At Pollok Country Park in Glasgow, Michael joins a women’s running club, the Bellahouston Harriers, for a warm-up session. In parkland above the pits en route to Cuningar Loop, he finds Glasgow’s geoenergy observatory, where the British Geological Society is researching how mine water heated naturally underground can be harnessed to save energy. At Livingston North, Michael investigates a puzzling incident in 1979 in Dechmont Woods. And at Edinburgh’s famous 12th-century castle, he discovers the origins of a spectacle that has attracted tourists from far and wide since 1950, the Edinburgh Tattoo.

programme.title: Walking With...
programme.desc: Famous for waking up the nation on Radio One and his afternoon drive-time show, DJ and presenter Nick Grimshaw heads to the north east of England for a stroll through a fascinating landscape. Exploring historic areas of Warkworth and Amble in Northumberland, Nick begins his walk at first light, dipping his toes in the sea for a refreshing wake-me-up along the beach. Crossing sand dunes and an ancient river, Nick heads for the streets of Warkworth and reflects on the importance of family life, including the positive influence of his late father. Nick talks about recognising his sexuality as he was growing up - including questioning what ‘being gay’ would actually mean. Taking the short boat crossing to the extraordinary Warkworth Hermitage - a retreat carved from a sandstone cliff - Nick talks about the benefits meditation has brought to his life. As Nick approaches the end of his walk, the conversation turns to the future. He talks passionately about his desire to start his own family with his partner. Revitalised after his seven-mile walk, Nick enjoys the sunset at Amble pier and vows to return to Northumberland.

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Historian Dan Snow explores the greatest maritime archaeology project in British history - the Mary Rose. Using 40 years of BBC archive footage Dan charts how the Mary Rose was discovered, excavated and eventually raised, and what the latest research has revealed about this iconic ship and her crew. Dan also investigates how the Mary Rose project helped create modern underwater archaeology, examining the techniques, challenges and triumphs of the divers and archaeologists involved.

programme.title: The Magic of Mushrooms
programme.desc: Professor Richard Fortey delves into the fascinating and normally hidden kingdom of fungi. From their spectacular birth, through a secretive underground life to their final explosive death, Richard reveals a remarkable world that few of us understand or even realise exists - yet all life on earth depends on it. In a specially built mushroom lab, with the help of mycologist Dr Patrick Hickey and some state-of-the-art technology, Richard brings to life the secret world of mushrooms as never seen before and reveals the spectacular abilities of fungi to break down waste and sustain new plant life, keeping our planet alive. Beyond the lab, Richard travels across Britain and beyond to show us the biggest, fastest and most deadly organisms on the planet - all of them fungi. He reveals their almost magical powers that have world-changing potential - opening up new frontiers in science, medicine and technology.

programme.title: Play for Today
programme.desc: Second series of the long-running strand of one-off dramas first broadcast in 1971/72.

programme.title: Private Life of a Masterpiece
programme.desc: Whistler's portrait of his elderly Puritan mother is one of the most satirised paintings of all time. Yet when his sombre masterpiece was first unveiled, it was seen as a radical departure from the sentimental images beloved of the Victorians, and which Whistler loathed. How did the portrait, born of Whistler's complex relationship with his mother, change the course of painting so drastically?

programme.title: This is BBC Four

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: At the former Midlothian mining village of Newtongrange, Michael meets the son of a miner whose name loomed large in the disputes of the 1970s and 1980s, 'Red' Mick McGahey. On the outskirts of Edinburgh, at a recording studio in South Gyle, Michael is transported back to the wild world of 1970s pop with Bay City Rollers guitarist Stuart 'Woody' Wood. Michael leaves Edinburgh and takes the train north to Dalmeny for Queensferry, on the banks of the Firth of Forth. At the Progress Rail foundry, he marvels at the traditional skills still required to produce complex sets of rails for modern tracks in Britain and across the world. On the south bank of the Firth of Forth, Michael recalls the construction during the 1950s and 1960s of the Forth Road Bridge, which he saw as a child from the train window while crossing the rail bridge.

programme.title: Walking With...
programme.desc: Comedian and author Shappi Khorsandi walks through stunning scenery in the Peak District and along the Monsal Trail. From the hills above Tideswell, Shappi follows tracks and footpaths along Tideswell Dale and Miller’s Dale before reaching the disused railway line of the Monsal Trail. Along the way she meets residents who are charmed by her characteristic wit and ease. Taking time to appreciate the hills and ridges surrounding her, Shappi enjoys a sense of calm and composure in the country air. Walking along the trail as it tunnels through hillsides and over gorges, Shappi takes the opportunity to ‘allow the hills to hold’ her. She talks candidly about her outlook on life and love. Funny and poignant, the comedian speaks of relationships, motherhood and mental health with a refreshing honesty.

programme.title: Jesus Christ Superstar
programme.desc: A novel take on the last seven days of Jesus Christ's life, based on Tim Rice and Andrew Lloyd Webber's famous rock opera. A group of young people arrive in the Holy Land by bus to act out events leading to the Crucifixion in a blend of biblical and contemporary themes. To Judas Iscariot, Christ is a badly managed superstar, bound to lose the support of a fickle public. To the priests, he is a menace, and to Mary Magdalene, he brings a love she has never known before. Photographed against dramatic scenery in Israel, the film is performed without dialogue and includes the hit songs I Don't Know How to Love Him, Superstar and King Herod's Song.

programme.title: Norman Jewison: Hollywood Veteran
programme.desc: Tim Sebastian talks to director and producer Norman Jewison, whose acclaimed films include In the Heat of the Night, Fiddler on the Roof and Moonstruck.

programme.title: In the Heat of the Night
programme.desc: Oscar-winning thriller about a bigoted sheriff in a small Mississippi cotton town who finds himself forced into working with a black homicide expert from Philadelphia.

programme.title: Scene by Scene
programme.desc: Rod Steiger talks to Mark Cousins about his celebrated roles in On the Waterfront, Dr Zhivago and In the Heat of the Night, and shares anecdotes about James Dean, Marilyn Monroe and Elizabeth Taylor.

programme.title: Castles: Britain's Fortified History
programme.desc: Sam Willis looks at the history of the castle from its first appearance with the Normans in 1066 to the longest siege on English soil at Kenilworth Castle 200 years later. The castle arrived as an instrument of invasion but soon became a weapon with which unruly barons challenged the Crown. Tintagel Castle, the place where King Arthur is said to have been conceived, is also on the itinerary. It remains one of the most evocative of castles to this day, drawing visitors from around the world with its tales of myth and legend.

programme.title: Scene by Scene
programme.desc: Rod Steiger talks to Mark Cousins about his celebrated roles in On the Waterfront, Dr Zhivago and In the Heat of the Night, and shares anecdotes about James Dean, Marilyn Monroe and Elizabeth Taylor.

programme.title: BBC Proms
programme.desc: Dazzling new classical arrangements of ELP, Genesis, Mike Oldfield, Yes and more, with performances from Guy Garvey, Gruff Rhys and Jane Weaver. Performing alongside the BBC Concert Orchestra and conductor Robert Ames, more is more in this ultimate rock-classical crossover. Expect synthesisers and tubular bells in a specially curated evening presented by Stuart Maconie.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 18 February 2000 and featuring Gabrielle, Daphne & Celeste, Hepburn, Joey Negro and Taka Boom, Eiffel 65, Ian Brown, DJ Luck & MC Neat and Oasis.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Simon Bates presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 22 July 1982 and featuring The Belle Stars, Madness, Bananarama, The Brat, Trio, Junior, The Stranglers, Dollar and Irene Cara.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Peter Powell present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 23 July 1986. Featuring Hollywood Beyond, Sinitta, Audrey Hall, Robert Palmer, Haywoode and Madonna.

programme.title: Prog at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of some of the greatest names and British bands in prog rock, filmed live in the BBC studios in the early 1970s. Featuring stadium names like Yes, Genesis and Emerson, Lake and Palmer alongside much-loved bands of the era, including Caravan, Family, Atomic Rooster and more.

programme.title: The Old Grey Whistle Test
programme.desc: Anne Nightingale introduces a recording of a Genesis concert performed at the Lyceum Ballroom, London, on 7 May 1980. The band is fronted by a bearded Phil Collins.

programme.title: The Lively Arts
programme.desc: A film, first broadcast in 1980, that portrays the many-sided talents of hugely successful British rock group Jethro Tull - at home, in the recording studio and on tour in America.

programme.title: Tech Now
programme.desc: Ammie Sekhon heads behind the scenes of WWE to see the tech that takes the show around the world. Shiona McCallum explores the latest sleep analysing technology. We meet the professor using biomechanics to help women find the best bra and tackle breast pain. And Andrew Rogers tests out the AI coming to the dentist's chair.

programme.title: AI Decoded
programme.desc: AI can write, code, plan and problem-solve in seconds. But as we hand more tasks over to machines, what happens to our own skills? In this episode, AI Decoded investigates whether AI is enhancing human intelligence or quietly eroding it. From doctors and software engineers to students growing up with AI tutors, we explore new research suggesting that reliance on AI could affect critical thinking, expertise and decision-making. Joining Marc are leading paediatric neurosurgeon Owase Jeelani, digital transformation researcher Dr Tapani Rinta-Kahila and AI Decoded co-host Dr Stephanie Hare.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: The Trump administration has issued subpoenas to several New York Times journalists following reporting about alleged security concerns involving President Trump's new Air Force One, a plane donated by Qatar. The case has prompted debate over the balance between protecting national security and safeguarding press freedom. Claire Atkinson, founder of The Media Mix newsletter, outlines the latest developments. The BBC's latest annual report comes as the corporation warns that public service media is facing a period of jeopardy. The figures show a further fall in licence fee-paying households, alongside changing audience habits and continued financial pressures as charter renewal negotiations continue. Rhodri Talfan Davies, the BBC's deputy director-general, sets out the corporation's position. Broadcaster and author Iain Dale reflects on a career spanning politics, publishing, blogging and radio following the publication of his memoir Have I Said Too Much?. The interview covers the enduring appeal of the radio phone-in, the influence of social media on broadcasting, and changing relationships between politicians and traditional media.

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle celebrate 25 years together on Animal Park, looking back at some of the animals, keepers and unforgettable moments they have shared, while new stories continue to unfold across the park. Following the loss of one of their brothers, the park's African wild dogs are struggling to find their place within the pack. With the social hierarchy unsettled, the keepers closely monitor their behaviour and introduce a special enrichment challenge, hoping it will help strengthen the bonds between the remaining dogs. As part of the anniversary celebrations, Ben and Kate revisit the remarkable life of Nico, the park's much-loved silverback gorilla. From his extraordinary bond with keeper Mark Tye to the illness that threatened his life and the legacy he left behind, they reflect on one of Animal Park's most unforgettable residents. Elsewhere, Megan discovers what the park's new maned wolves make of some unusual treats, while an extraordinary scientific project could see the legacy of beloved lioness Malaika helping to shape future exploration on Mars.

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A man is found dead in his flat, and he has been robbed. Police trace the last person he called on his mobile phone. But when toxicology tests reveal the victim died after drinking a deadly cocktail, police are under pressure to find the evidence to prove their prime suspect is a killer. And in another case, police trying to crack open an organised drugs gang operating out of a tandoori takeaway hit the jackpot when they discover the owner is renting a warehouse in a neighbouring county.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: A forensic scientist and a fingerprint expert identify a killer nearly 60 years after the crime, and a plant scientist helps expose a fraudster in a fake tea-growing operation.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flashback to 2016, when Dion visited a former police station in Birmingham that wasn’t your usual auction lot. Things are a bit more conventional for him in Liverpool at a three-bedroom mid-terrace, but there’s nothing 'standard' about Jacqui’s property in Hertfordshire.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Our two teams are bargain-hunting at an antiques fair in the historic market town of Newbury, where Caroline Hawley also chats to fairgoers to see if they can guess our mystery item. Our teams, both mother-and-daughter combinations, are Katie and Eden, guided by Raj Bisram, and Ni and Beth, aided by Stephanie Connell. Tasked to find an item you would find on a dining table, the reds have their eyes set on a silver-plated cloche, but will this reveal a profit! Meanwhile, Stephanie Connell helps the blues hunt for an item with a connection to birds as they have their eyes set on a pair of metal garden bird ornaments, but will they fly? As the clock counts down, can the reds strike an excellent deal on a Maurice Lacroix wristwatch, and the blues are left to make an important decision. Down the road in Newbury, Caroline meets auctioneer Thomas Forrester to get his estimates on the teams’ items before they go under Thomas’s gavel, but how will they do?

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: A British rookie cop tackles crime during her first day on duty in paradise, and a traffic patrol officer tries to keep his learner driver son safe on Bermuda’s roads.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Australia
programme.desc: Rick Stein travels south from the dry outback into the Riverina, a region that defies Australia’s reputation as the world’s most arid inhabited continent.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is exploring Herefordshire and Gloucestershire, helping a couple make the move from Germany to the British countryside. Alistair shows them a fantastic mystery house – a smallholding which comes complete with Tom, Barbara, Margo and Jerry, four goats who are already living the good life on the property. Alistair also meets up with a writer who exchanged London life for Herefordshire’s green calm and now shares her lovely, inspiring home with a menagerie of animals.

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presides as members of the public with interesting items to sell come face to face with five dealers who bid against each other to try to bag themselves a bargain. Before facing the dealers, each seller has their item valued by auctioneer Simon Bower. Simon also gives them top tips on the history and key characteristics of their piece, so that when they enter the bidding room, they have all the information they need to try to haggle the best possible price from the dealers. All the dealers are spending their own money but have no idea what the sellers have brought in.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: With JB and Lauren’s help, Tanya lets go of her parents’ treasured items to fund a dream trip to India, thus honouring her father’s memory and reconnecting with her heritage.

programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns - but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Gyles Brandreth. As always, contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: For centuries, horses were integral to our working landscape, but horse logger Kate Mobbs-Morgan believes they still have a key role to play in our woodlands.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian prepares for his court date, and Linda is on a mission to save the community centre.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Horologist Steve Fletcher takes on a cleverly designed accessory with a unique purpose, while Mark tackles a vintage projector holding long-buried family memories of life in Uganda.

programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: A secret relationship cracks suspect Samak’s story open. Meanwhile, in a new case in Ross-on-Wye, the discovery of a woman's body sparks a frantic search for her missing mother.

programme.title: Joins BBC News

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle celebrate 25 years together on Animal Park, looking back at some of the animals, keepers and unforgettable moments they have shared, while new stories continue to unfold across the park. It's an emotional time in Wolf Wood as beloved male wolf Terry falls seriously ill. Faced with a heartbreaking decision, the keepers say goodbye to him, leaving his mate Meg alone just as she may be carrying his pups. Days later, when Meg disappears into her den and refuses to emerge, fears grow for her safety and the future of Terry's legacy. As part of the anniversary celebrations, Ben and Kate look back at one of the park's most memorable stories, the ingenious homemade wheel that transformed the life of three-legged tortoise Winky and captured the hearts of viewers. Elsewhere, Kate catches up with the park's new hippos as they continue settling into their purpose-built home, while Megan puts some of the park's longest tongues to the test and joins the rhino keepers for an up-close health check.

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: Two young women are shot dead while out celebrating New Year’s Eve in Birmingham. The crime bears all the hallmarks of a gangland shooting, but neither woman has any connection to the gangs. They are innocent victims, and it makes police all the more determined to catch their killers. They launch an audacious undercover operation to infiltrate rival gangs, but will they find the moment of proof they need to arrest the killer? And a thief is caught on camera stealing from a Royal Mail van in broad daylight. Despite the footage, police struggle to identify him... until a police community service officer from a neighbouring force recognises the person in the images.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: A forensic biologist works tirelessly to match DNA to the killer of Julia James, and an entomologist links a killer to a double murder.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A trip down memory lane for Martin in Hampshire results in a whimsical tale about 'half a house'. In Kent, Jacqui sees a property that could be resurrected into an HMO. And Dion returns to a Stafford site with great potential that he first saw in 2023.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Myth and mystery surround this episode of the show with Charlie Ross. At the Museum of Gloucester, Charlie learns about artefacts found in the grave of a high-status woman from the late Iron Age or early Roman period. Discovered by quarrymen in the Birdlip area of Gloucestershire in 1879, the items include an ornate bowl, beads from a necklace and a world-famous bronze mirror. Further north, in Powys, two teams of bargain hunters scour the stalls of an antiques fair in Builth Wells. They are mum-and-daughter duos Babs and Rachel and Laurel and Georgina. With 60 minutes on the clock, the reds – guided by expert Kate Bliss – spot a Victorian caddy, but they decide it’s not their cup of tea. The blues, meanwhile, take a fancy to a wooden foo dog but dismiss it when they see how pricey the ‘pooch’ is. With speedy Tim Weeks as their expert, the blue team finish their shop first and pass the time by having a walking race. You’ll have to watch to see who wins.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: Police are on the hunt for lawbreakers inside an ecological nature reserve, we go underwater with the scuba-diving unit, and officers break up a neighbourhood dispute.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Australia
programme.desc: Rick Stein’s journey around New South Wales takes him from the country’s interior to the Sapphire Coast.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In north Wales, Jules Hudson helps an adventurous buyer turn a favourite holiday destination into his forever home by finding a dog-friendly house with countryside walks on the doorstep. Jules also does his bit for ‘citizen science’ when he heads to the coast to learn how the public can safely monitor and protect the region’s seal population.

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presides as members of the public with interesting items to sell come face to face with five dealers who bid against each other to try to bag themselves a bargain. Before facing the dealers, each seller has their item valued by auctioneer Simon Bower. Simon also gives them top tips on the history and key characteristics of their piece, so that when they enter the bidding room, they have all the information they need to try to haggle the best possible price from the dealers. All the dealers are spending their own money but have no idea what the sellers have brought in.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Michael's life took an unexpected turn when his wife Jan started losing her sight due to a rare eye condition. As they adjusted to their new reality, they decided to downsize to a more accessible home. However, Michael's prized antique collection posed a significant problem – their new home lacked the space to accommodate it all. As a temporary measure, much of the collection ended up in the home of Jan's son, but now his loft and garage are full. Michael has decided the time has come to part with the items he has taken decades to collect, so he has called in the Travelling Auctioneers to help. After a tough few years, the family would love a holiday – but how much will the funds made at auction contribute towards getting them onto a cruise ship? Auctioneer JB has just a day to uncover the items worthy of taking to auction, while restorer Lauren must restore worn pieces so they’re ready to go under the hammer. JB is convinced that a milliner's hat block with ties to the region's history will attract high bids, as well as a selection of music-themed lots. Lauren gives sympathetic restoration to a unique chair and revives an old artist's easel.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns - but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Gyles Brandreth. As always, contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Jamie McGregor and the passionate community helping to revive Scotland’s ancient tradition of stone lifting. Once used as a test of strength, stone lifting is being reimagined for modern Scotland. Open to everyone – women and men, seasoned athletes and complete beginners alike – it has become a small but fiercely supportive community. The challenge is physical, but the reward is deeply emotional. One of the country’s most experienced stone lifters, Jamie is a prison officer, former Strongman competitor and tireless advocate for the sport. During a packed few months, he heads out on stone-lifting tours through Perthshire and Iceland, places a new lifting stone on an ancient Highland coffin road, celebrates his 50th birthday, and prepares to marry his fiancee Gail – affectionately known as his 'stone wife' – who has supported him throughout his stone-lifting journey. Along the way, we meet the fun and colourful characters who make up this close-knit 'stone family'. A warm and uplifting portrait of strength, community and heritage, this documentary explores how an ancient test of power is finding new life in the modern world.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Elaine decides to play matchmaker, and Ian quizzes Lauren over his suspicions.

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Two women lost in time: an enigmatic Indian woman and an 18th-century Scottish artist. Will this quest uncover a double attribution leading to a six-figure price tag?

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Adeel Akhtar wants to know how his ancestors came from India to Kenya and makes a surprising discovery that his roots in the country run deeper than he ever imagined.

programme.title: BBC London

programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: When Jo is found stabbed at home, her husband is arrested. As forensic evidence and witness accounts are examined, detectives grow highly sceptical of his story.

programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: A secret relationship cracks suspect Samak’s story open. Meanwhile, in a new case in Ross-on-Wye, the discovery of a woman's body sparks a frantic search for her missing mother.

programme.title: Alan Carr's Picture Slam
programme.desc: Comedian Alan Carr returns to host the fun and frantic quiz show where every question is a picture and every picture is worth cash. They could be anything from famous faces, places, products or logos, and teams need to name them all for a Picture Slam. Playing this time are friends Selin and Harin from London, cousins Gerard and Jeremy from South Wales and Kev and Bex, a couple from Motherwell. They battle it out across four rounds, shouting out answers and building their banks. But only one team can make it to the final Super Slam for the chance to win £10,000.

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle celebrate 25 years together on Animal Park, looking back at some of the animals, keepers and unforgettable moments they have shared, while new stories continue to unfold across the park. Young sea lion Rocky takes the biggest step of his life as he leaves the safety of his training area and joins the rest of the colony out on Half Mile Pond. As he finds his place alongside the adult sea lions and the park's newly arrived hippos, the keepers hope months of training will help him adapt to life in the open water. As part of the anniversary celebrations, Ben revisits the remarkable story of the park's Amur tiger cubs as Ginger and Seeka prepare to leave for new homes, while the team also celebrates 60 years since the safari park first opened its gates, transforming the way people experienced wildlife in Britain. Elsewhere, Megan meets the park's new hippos, Jon devises a new feeding challenge for the Cuban crocodiles, and the lionesses enjoy a very special birthday celebration.

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: West Midlands Police are facing a massive spate of burglaries, and they know one man is responsible for them all. But they just can’t catch him in the act. However quickly police arrive, the intruder seems to have vanished into thin air. The break-ins continue unabated - one of the detectives even gets burgled himself! But a single piece of crucial information from a police informant finally gives officers the upper hand. Also, the National Crime Agency is alerted to a plumber who’s buying gun-making equipment online. Could there be an innocent explanation, or is something unusual going on in his lock-up?

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: A skilled voice analyst proves the killer of a missing man is very close to home, and a UK fingerprint expert helps to overturn a miscarriage of justice.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Programme in which experts uncover the various tricks of the property auction trade

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles meets a collector with a thirst for preserving brewery history. In a converted garage in Nottinghamshire, Richard Percival has 1,300 beer trays, dating back more than 100 years. Made of heavy steel with ornate designs, they’re all striking examples of British advertising – and Eric Knowles has arranged a surprise visit for him, to recognise his record-breaking collection! In nearby Newark, two teams hope their items will serve up a profit when they go shopping with their antiques experts. Nick Hall is browsing bargains with the reds, Gary and Ian, and Izzie Balmer is with the blues, Joe and Hazel. With 60 minutes on the clock, the red team spots a pot with a rim made by a well-known silversmith and decide it’s one to think about. Meanwhile, the blues are mixing things up with a pestle and mortar. In Derbyshire, it’s almost time for last orders as auctioneer Charles Hanson gets the bids flowing.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Hairdresser Mysteries
programme.desc: When a celebrity weather reporter is found dead, Lily investigates if his zero-star reviews of all the local businesses have driven one of their own to murder.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Australia
programme.desc: Rick travels north along his favourite coastline as he recalls the catastrophic bushfires of the Black Summer of 2019 which ravaged this area of the South Coast.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple want to swap their Cheshire townhouse for the dark skies and beautiful open spaces of Shropshire. Denise Nurse helps them with the property search and also visits a flower farm in full bloom to witness at first hand how modern technology is working hand-in-hand with nature.

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presides as members of the public with interesting items to sell come face to face with five dealers who bid against each other to try to bag themselves a bargain. Before facing the dealers, each seller has their item valued by auctioneer Simon Bower. Simon also gives them top tips on the history and key characteristics of their piece, so that when they enter the bidding room, they have all the information they need to try to haggle the best possible price from the dealers. All the dealers are spending their own money but have no idea what the sellers have brought in.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Izzie and Saf clear a bungalow of quirky collectibles, including an Egyptian sarcophagus and a suffragette mannequin. However, they lose track of the time during a cuckoo clock fix.

programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns - but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Gyles Brandreth. As always, contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Atlantic bluefin tuna, one of the largest fish in the sea, are making a surprise comeback off Northern Ireland’s coast, sparking an ambitious new vision for local fishing. Davy Frayne and Jonny Thursby, two anglers from the town of Ballycastle, have been fishing for giant bluefin tuna in the area for six years. Now they are attempting to establish a bluefin fishery, boasting catches of up to 1,000 pounds in weight. The stretch between Ballycastle and Rathlin is an unforgiving one, so Davy and Jonny are tested to their limits, fishing in waters where bad weather, strong currents and deep waters can create treacherous conditions. They are on the water at every opportunity, starting at the break of dawn and fishing until the light fades over the water to the west. Ballycastle is a town steeped in fishing history, where the people have longstanding connections with the waters. But in recent years, fleets have diminished and fish stocks have been low. Davy and Jonny are attempting to bring a new form of fishing to this coastline, one never seen before in these waters, and many sceptics doubt the viability of the project.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Izzie and Robin get rummaging and restoring in York. Could a tractor, a horse and a whole heap of toy cars help a special little girl get the treatment she needs?

programme.title: Granite Harbour
programme.desc: A birthday celebration ends in tragedy when a girl is found dead near a traveller site. As tensions rise, Lindo and the team uncover secrets that threaten to tear a community apart.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon is back in the host’s chair for the award-winning comedy panel show, with Lee Mack and David Mitchell returning as the lightning-quick team captains. Each week, a stellar cast of celebrity guests reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up? Sorting the fact from the fiction this week are guest panellists Nabil Abdulrashid, Lucy Beaumont, Gethin Jones and Francesca Mills.

programme.title: Ann Droid
programme.desc: Sue isn’t thrilled that Michael is back with Renee, his cheating wife, and turns down an invite to Renee’s BJJ graduation ceremony, pretending she has other plans and upsetting Michael in the process. Sue doesn’t have any plans, so offers to work in the charity shop to help out Eileen, but it’s a disaster. Sue realises she should go and support Michael after all and prove she’s still a good mum, but Linda causes chaos.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Austin
programme.desc: Austin’s rock star persona spirals, Mel faces scrutiny over her sex life, and Ingrid discovers who’s secretly writing Big Bear.

programme.title: Austin
programme.desc: After appearing on a controversial podcast and getting Haley fired, Austin faces a family intervention. Meanwhile, Ingrid is auditioning the whole of Canberra to find an actor to play Big Bear before she loses the show. The last audition, a late entry, may be the one.

programme.title: La La Land
programme.desc: Mia (Emma Stone), an aspiring actress, and Sebastian (Ryan Gosling), a dedicated jazz musician, fall in love while struggling to make ends meet in a city known for crushing hopes and breaking hearts. Set in modern-day Los Angeles, this award-winning musical about everyday life explores the joy and pain of pursuing your dreams.

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: After the attack on his best friends and threats against his sister, Mobeen is armed, dangerous and out for revenge. But can he solve his problems without any bloodshed, or will they land him deeper in the criminal world he’s trying to escape. His attempts to make up for past mistakes see him pulled back into the world of unhinged crime boss Uncle Khan. Owing money to Khan (with deadly consequences if he doesn’t pay him back), Mobeen struggles to undertake his criminal orders under the watchful eye of Khan’s useless nephew Nav. Now living at Uncle Shady’s 'safe house', can Mobeen keep his family safe whilst staying out of jail? Or will the dark path he is being forced down lead to him paying the ultimate price?

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: Mobeen tries to do a good deed by helping out at the local food bank, but juggling this with strict orders from Uncle Khan to launder money could leave him with a deadly debt that is impossible to pay.

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: Mobeen and Aks must demonstrate their commitment to a good cause to prevent her being excluded from school. This involves attending the funeral of Mobeen’s former teacher - but when drug a deal goes wrong at the church, Mobeen has bigger things to worry about.

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: With a hit out on Officer Harper, Mobeen steps in to try to save his life and take him out of the firing line. Eight and Nate must stall Harper’s assassin long enough for Mobeen and Harper to escape through the woods.

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: Mobeen and the gang must get out of Small Heath, but can he tie up loose ends and guarantee his sister’s safety before the police or Khan’s gang take him down?

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: We’ve travelled to the heart of the south Wales valleys to Pontypridd and Y Muni, a multipurpose, fully accessible live music and comedy venue. Louie McLean, Suri Ashish, Sarah Roberts, Paul Hilleard and Josh Harvey as Bernard Guff will take to the stage in a bid to impress our judges and book their place in the final. Welsh comedian Kiri Pritchard-McLean is our host for this heat. Joining her is our panel of judges, actor and comic Spencer Jones, and Hannah Moulder, producer of the award-winning Juice on BBC Three. Head judge Babatunde Aleshe completes the trio. It’s not going to be easy for them to pick a winner from the five new comics performing. With only five minutes of stage time, the sets are jam-packed with jokes and cutting-edge material, but only one can go through to the grand final in November at Birmingham’s historic Alexandra Theatre.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: An oddball family make a brave, but bungling, attempt to save their failing funeral home in the sleepy Scottish fishing village of Kincully. Archie, having recently inherited the floundering family business from his domineering dad, is determined to prove himself. His sister Moira, the company mortician, and hearse driver Cousin Ron are looking to Archie to prevent A Better Place Funeral Home from falling into the hands of Sympathy Plus, a corporate funeral chain run by the power-hungry Eileen. Archie is also living in the shadow of his late father - literally. He keeps him in a great big freezer on the landing. With time running out, Archie hatches a plan to keep hold of the family business. Will Archie’s scheme work? Or will A Better Place fall prey to Eileen’s growing empire?

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Adventurer Steve Backshall and the Deadly 60 team pack their cold weather gear and head north to Alaska. They battle for days and nights in the freezing conditions of the frozen tundra, hoping to find polar bears to add to the list. They are rewarded with a rare sighting of a wolverine and some cheeky Arctic foxes. Steve has heard of a place where thousands of bald eagles arrive every year to feed on the spawning salmon. Keen to see them first-hand, he heads up one of the freezing rivers to get a closer look. Alaska is also home to the grizzly bear, and Steve won't be going home until he has seen one and added it to his list.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall and the team brave the intense heat of the Arizona desert as they go in search of more incredible animals for the Deadly 60 list. In order to demonstrate the awesome hunting skills of the Harris hawk, Steve becomes their prey for the day. Some state-of-the-art technology helps Steve track down deadly rattlesnakes and a real monster. It is then off to the mountains in search of a very elusive cat, the cougar. After seeing pictures captured by remote cameras, the team know the cats are there, but can they find them?

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson celebrates the 50th birthday of the Jaguar E-type by driving a modern half-a-million pound interpretation of this classic sports car before putting on a typically low-key birthday party involving live music, classic fighter planes and a group of Royal Marines. Meanwhile, Richard Hammond is in South Africa to test a bigger, even more rugged alternative to the famous Hummer, while James May meets up with gold medal winning British Olympian Amy Williams for a wintry test of the latest Mini rally car. Plus the power crazed BMW 1 series M is on the test track and another top celebrity guest takes their place in the Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Las Vegas has reinvented itself to pull in a new generation of visitors - recasting the city away from being the home of gambling to the world’s go-to destination for live entertainment. In this two-part series, reporter Tir Dhondy finds out how the city has changed, what this new improved version of Vegas offers today’s visitors, and how this shift is being felt by the people who live and work there.

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Las Vegas has reinvented itself to pull in a new generation of visitors - recasting the city away from being the home of gambling to the world’s go-to destination for live entertainment. In this two-part series, reporter Tir Dhondy finds out how the city has changed, what this new improved version of Vegas offers today’s visitors, and how this shift is being felt by the people who live and work there.

programme.title: Zara McDermott
programme.desc: With access to luxury clubs, yachts and villas, as well as police, undercover detectives and emergency medics, Zara McDermott discovers the secrets of this unique island.

programme.title: Zara McDermott
programme.desc: Zara gets a closer look at how social media is changing Ibiza – and its crime.

programme.title: Zara McDermott
programme.desc: Zara McDermott comes face to face with the criminal economy that keeps the drugs flowing in Ibiza and sees how police on the front line cope with this constant flow of Class As.

programme.title: Zara McDermott
programme.desc: Zara gets tantalisingly close to the top of the drugs network in Ibiza.

programme.title: Roman Kemp: The Fight for Young Lives
programme.desc: On a daily basis, Roman Kemp is contacted by young people who are struggling. With people turning to him for advice, he’s questioning whether or not there is a worsening mental health crisis and asking if these young people should be offered more support in their own communities. This documentary follows Roman as he immerses himself in schools, youth centres and treatment settings around the country, meets with experts and charities, and visits the Houses of Parliament to try to understand the potential solutions for improving the mental health of young people in the UK today.

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: We head to sunny south London and the remarkable Rivoli Ballrooms, a world-famous venue that’s dripping in original vintage décor. Designed by Henley Attwater in 1913, it’s one of the last 1950s style ballrooms in the country. Five brand new acts take to the stage in front of a packed house of more than two hundred comedy lovers. Award-winning comedian Laura Smyth is the host, and joining her are the three judges – Mexican-born stand-up Lara Ricote, Motherland writer Holly Walsh and head judge, Babatunde Aleshe. Muhsin Yesilada, Maia Tassalini, Limahl Germain, Alfie Packham and Frankie Thompson each perform their set before the judges decide who will go through to the grand final. There can only be one winner, so who will it be?

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson celebrates the 50th birthday of the Jaguar E-type by driving a modern half-a-million pound interpretation of this classic sports car before putting on a typically low-key birthday party involving live music, classic fighter planes and a group of Royal Marines. Meanwhile, Richard Hammond is in South Africa to test a bigger, even more rugged alternative to the famous Hummer, while James May meets up with gold medal winning British Olympian Amy Williams for a wintry test of the latest Mini rally car. Plus the power crazed BMW 1 series M is on the test track and another top celebrity guest takes their place in the Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve and the team are on the search for deadly animals in the steaming swamps of the southern USA. The contenders are everywhere, not least the alligators that live in just about every pond and lake. But the first animal on the list is a complete surprise, a monster fish with two rows of sharp fangs that certainly keeps Steve on his toes. The crew find themselves up to their armpits in a swamp hoping to find a deadly snake with the unusual name of cottonmouth. Last but not least, Steve and his team come face to face with an enormous record-breaking predatory turtle.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Naturalist Steve Backshall heads to the Bahamas in order to get up close and personal with a whole bunch of sharks. There are plenty of shark species to choose from and Steve has his work cut out deciding which ones are worthy of a place on his Deadly 60 list. Hooking up with shark scientists helps him decide and gives him an opportunity to release some baby sharks into the wild, and he learns how to hypnotise them with his bare hands. Getting mobbed by hungry reef sharks and swimming with a four-metre tiger shark are all in a day's work for Steve and the team!

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: It's the ultimate test of man against machine or should that be dog against machine? Either way, it's Top Gear's most ambitious and arduous challenge to date. Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond attempt to race from the town of Resolute in northern Canada to the Magnetic North Pole, 450 miles away. The terrain in between is some of the toughest on earth - a mix of mountainous land masses and jagged sea ice where temperatures can drop to -65C. And then there are the polar bears - the Arctic is home to 80 per cent of the world's population, and when it comes to lunch they really don't care if it's seal or human. This being Top Gear, the three presenters' modes of transport are a little out of the ordinary. Jeremy and James drive a specially adapted pickup truck, whilst Richard travels on something a little less high-tech - a sled pulled by a team of ten Canadian Inuit dogs. Pitting dog power against horse power may sound like a one-sided contest, but given the nature of the terrain, there's no telling who will make it to the Pole first. In fact, there's no guarantee that either team will reach the finish line at all.

programme.title: The Traitors Canada
programme.desc: The guests are shocked to find four players absent from the breakfast table, only to learn that their fate in the game is at the risk of being buried. While the remaining players attempt to save them from a grave fate, they quickly discover that not everyone will make it out alive.

programme.title: The Traitors Canada
programme.desc: With suspicion intensifying inside the manor and a focus on one player in particular, another sees it as an opportunity to set their cunning plan into motion and make a swift strike for revenge.

programme.title: The Traitors Canada
programme.desc: In the aftermath of an eventful Round Table and banishment, the remaining guests begin to examine the exchanges between certain players the night before, while another finds themself stranded and at the mercy of the Traitors.

programme.title: The Traitors Canada
programme.desc: With a Faithful treading troubled waters with their fellow guests, the Traitors see an opportunity to set a trap disguised as an invitation to join their midst. The Traitors wait with bated breath to see if their plan will succeed or if the player figures out their malicious intent.

programme.title: My Old Ass
programme.desc: In this coming-of-age fable, free-spirited teenager Elliott encounters her wisecracking 39-year-old future self and can’t escape her influence. On the cusp of leaving home for college, Elliott is determined to have one last summer packed with good times. To celebrate her 18th birthday, she boats off to a remote island with her best friends to trip on magic mushrooms. But just when Elliott thinks she’s not feeling a thing, she suddenly finds herself chatting with a woman who claims to be her literal 'old ass', the person she is apparently destined to become in two decades' time! Prodded for advice, Elliott’s future self gives her one major warning: avoid anyone named Chad, which sounds easy enough. But when Elliott meets the very Chad she was supposed to dodge, things get complicated. Elliott starts to open herself to what her future self might have to show her... and vice versa!

programme.title: Hunting the Rolex Rippers
programme.desc: More and more luxury watches are being snatched off UK streets. In 2022, there was an estimated 60 per cent increase in watch thefts worldwide. It is now the crime of choice for many criminals, seen as easier and more lucrative than drug dealing. In this documentary, journalist Tir Dhondy gets access, for the first time ever, to the gang members behind the crime. She meets the teenage moped riders snatching watches off the street, as well as a gang leader who runs a team of teenage watch rippers and female watch spotters. Tir also comes face-to-face with the 16-year-old who used a machete to steal singer Aled Jones’s £17,000 Rolex in broad daylight.

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Las Vegas has reinvented itself to pull in a new generation of visitors - recasting the city away from being the home of gambling to the world’s go-to destination for live entertainment. In this two-part series, reporter Tir Dhondy finds out how the city has changed, what this new improved version of Vegas offers today’s visitors, and how this shift is being felt by the people who live and work there.

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Las Vegas has reinvented itself to pull in a new generation of visitors - recasting the city away from being the home of gambling to the world’s go-to destination for live entertainment. In this two-part series, reporter Tir Dhondy finds out how the city has changed, what this new improved version of Vegas offers today’s visitors, and how this shift is being felt by the people who live and work there.
