programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: A helicopter trip over the vast expanse of Brazil's northern savannah helps adventurer Steve Backshall find his next contender for the Deadly 60. He is in search of the elusive giant anteater, but can he get close enough to film one for his list? A chance encounter with a tough little owl with an ingenious way of catching its supper captures Steve's attention and the rainforest lives up to its name as a very wet team ventures out at night looking for some of the world's deadliest bugs.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall and the Deadly crew find themselves in deep water as they head into the flooded forests of Brazil. The water is teeming with life and Steve takes a swim in a piranha-infested river in order to find out more about these killer fish. They are assured of a place on the Deadly 60 list until other mysterious fish lurking in the depths make Steve think again. Determined to see one of the strangest animals that inhabit the waters of the flooded forest, Steve and the team go in search of pink river dolphins, and an encounter with one of the most venomous snakes on the planet rounds off this flooded forest adventure.

programme.title: Horrible Histories: The Movie
programme.desc: When Roman lad Atti offends edgy Emperor Nero, he is drafted to fight in far-flung Britain, where local Celt lass Orla itches to join Boudicca’s uprising against the occupying legions. Comedy from BBC Films.

programme.title: Ludwig
programme.desc: John and the gang go back to school to investigate the suspicious death of a headmaster. In doing so, John is faced with his own bad memories of school but is pleasantly surprised when he reconnects with his old mentor, the man who taught him everything about puzzles. With John distracted by the case, Lucy follows a new lead which she hopes will bring her closer to finding James. Lucy’s covert mission takes her to Wales, but she’s not the lone wolf she thinks she is, and her discoveries leave her rattled. A shocking revelation from IT consultant Holly forces John to question everything.

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Angry, pervy, outrageous and hilarious, Fleabag arrives with a bang as she spins through the city grasping at anyone and anything that might keep her head above water.

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Haunted by memories of her dead best friend, dumped by her emotionally fragile boyfriend and now desperately trying to sell her stolen goods, Fleabag attempts to rekindle romantic fires to distract her from the mayhem of her life.

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Fleabag helps her inappropriate brother-in-law buy a present for Claire, who is organising her own surprise birthday party. On the hunt for a date, Fleabag reconnects with a toothy friend.

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Fleabag and her sister Claire reluctantly visit a female-only silent retreat, courtesy of their father. Their enforced silence is interrupted by an unusual neighbouring weekend workshop, explosive secrets coming to light and there is a surprising connection with an old acquaintance.

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: On the anniversary of her mother's death, Fleabag and Claire return to their family home for the annual memorial lunch. Although this time there is an interloper... Things get heated between Fleabag and her godmother, who muscles her way into the occasion and reveals plans for her sex-hibition.

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Fleabag takes a date to her godmother's Sex-hibition to discover a few shocks are in store for her there, including a declaration of love, a familiar piece of work and an entire wall of surprises. But where is Claire? The season finale forces Fleabag to face up to her actions and to finally reveal what happened to her beloved friend Boo.

programme.title: My Epic Camel Adventure with Gordon Buchanan
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan takes an epic journey across the Gobi Desert on one of the world’s most iconic, yet least understood, animals – camels. Starting in the west of Mongolia, Gordeon crosses blistering sand dunes, endless rocky plains and the treacherous Bumbat Mountain, with only his camels for company. If he is to succeed, he must befriend the camels, learn as much as he can about them, and try to win their trust and respect. Without that, the journey and his survival are in question. Naming camels isn’t something that nomads would do, but as his relationship with them strengthens, he decides to name them Squeaks and Chewy. They are lucky enough to meet some of the other inhabitants of this seemingly desolate landscape, such as wolves, saiga antelopes and yaks. But will he be lucky enough to catch sight of the most endangered bear on the planet – the Gobi Bear? Spending time with different nomads also gives Gordon time to reflect on the appeal of the traditional nomadic way of life, and his own modern existence. Ultimately, he discovers how camels have evolved in the most unusual ways to survive and prosper in one of the most hostile environments on the planet.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah is in the Peak District helping Andrew, and his faithful dog Edna, swap Amsterdam for a home in the UK’s first National Park. Supported by his big sister, and with a budget of £375,000, he’s hoping for a characterful cottage with spectacular scenery. Property picks include homes boasting fantastic views and a Mystery House which comes with something special that should appeal to his sibling! Whilst there, Sonali also discovers how local woodlands are being transformed, as young native trees are creating a greener future following the impact of ash dieback.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: In Scotland, Izzie and Robin help Anne declutter her crammed flat to raise funds for a campervan adventure. But can a dishevelled Hindu deity bring them the fortunes they need?

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On board a sleek Shinkansen bullet train, Michael arrives in Tokyo, one of the greatest cities on earth, with a population of 37 million in the metropolitan area. He begins by exploring the ornate Akasaka Palace, built in 1909 by Emperor Meiji for his son, the crown prince Yoshihito. He’s surprised to find that, with its neo-baroque architecture and impressive fountains, it looks more than a little British. In the fashionable district of Ginza, Michael journeys underground to ride the Tokyo metro. He’s intrigued by the catchy jingles played at each station and meets one of the composers behind 300 of the tunes. Stopping off in the neighbourhood of Ueno to explore the famous Ameyoko street market, Michael travels to the east of the city to marvel at the Tokyo Skytree, standing at over 630 meters high. After a giddy ride to the top to take in the views, he learns how it was designed to be earthquake-proof.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a stunning railway adventure through the western Mediterranean islands of Sardinia and Corsica, beginning in Sardinia’s capital, Cagliari. Michael delves into the city's Roman past and experiences warm Sardinian hospitality with a taste of the traditional myrtle liqueur. He discovers the legacy of Eleanora de Arborea, the pioneering medieval ruler who shaped Sardinia’s laws, and listens to the unique sounds of the Sardinian language, Sardo, and its haunting choral singing. Travelling west to Porto Flavia, he marvels at an extraordinary feat of engineering, an underground railway and tunnel which revolutionised the transport of the island’s prized minerals to the Mediterranean.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Kemah Bob, JJ Chalmers, Kevin Clifton and Victoria Derbyshire take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games kitchen composter?

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp hosts this quiz show with sidekick Sarah Greene where contestants race on moving podiums across the studio floor. Correct answers get the players moving, while wrong answers will stop them in their tracks. Five contestants battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - make it to the finish line before your rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the £5,000 cash prize.

programme.title: Cricket: The Hundred
programme.desc: Coverage of The Hundred 100-ball cricket tournament.

programme.title: Cricket: The Hundred
programme.desc: Coverage of The Hundred 100-ball cricket tournament.

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: With Storm Eunice - and its 100 mph winds - battering the south coast, the Portsmouth crew have to decide whether they can even launch to a windsurfer in trouble. Once they’re on the water, the situation escalates when it’s reported that the casualty doesn’t have his diabetes medication with him. In Skerries, on the east coast of Ireland, a husband watches on as his wife struggles on her kayak. When the crew race to her rescue, they discover her in the water and close to exhaustion. The Exmouth crew find themselves in danger when they attempt to rescue a female wing foiler who’s trapped in the surf line. And in Sussex, Selsey’s all-weather lifeboat faces a real challenge in rescuing a yacht with rigging failure out in the English Channel.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: Uncanny
programme.desc: Was a young girl in rural Cambridgeshire visited by the apparition of an Edwardian school teacher? Were a teenage boy and his family in County Durham subjected to a vicious poltergeist attack in the 1980s? And were three Oxford students preyed upon by a malevolent force in the early 90s?

programme.title: Amityville: An Origin Story
programme.desc: After paranormal trailblazers Ed and Lorraine Warren 'confirm' a demonic presence at 112 Ocean Ave, the Lutz family turns their 'true story' into a hit book and film. Fame comes at a heavy cost when they are accused of perpetrating a massive hoax.

programme.title: Amityville: An Origin Story
programme.desc: The success of the Amityville Horror reinvents the haunted house for a new generation, spawning sequels, conspiracy theories and a rabid fandom. But as George Lutz's secret past is revealed, the human toll and absurd legacy of Amityville come into focus.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Comedian and presenter Susan Calman, internet star Max Fosh and DJ Noreen Khan are back for day 2 as the week moves on to its second stop. Vernon Kay keeps the quiz on track, while Traitors star Paul Gorton is back as an unreliable tour guide, turning the spotlight on Enniskillen and its surprising stories. With scores beginning to take shape, attention shifts towards who could become the week's overall champion.

programme.title: Scamanda
programme.desc: As Nancy and Detective Martinez dig deeper, the IRS steps in. Is Amanda's web of lies about to unravel?

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle celebrate 25 years together on Animal Park, looking back at some of the animals, keepers and unforgettable moments they have shared, while new stories continue to unfold across the park. After years of planning, it's a landmark day as four hippos take their first steps beyond their new home and into Half Mile Pond. The keepers have spent months preparing for this moment, but once the hippos venture into the water, there's no telling what they'll do next, or whether they'll come back when called. As part of the anniversary celebrations, Ben and Kate revisit the story of Kadu, one of the park's most loved Amur tigers. When a routine procedure uncovered devastating news, her keepers faced one of the hardest decisions of their careers in a story that remains one of the most emotional moments in Animal Park's history. Elsewhere, the Farmyard celebrates the arrival of adorable pygmy goat kids, while Megan McCubbin meets a critically endangered cotton-top tamarin who has recently given birth to a precious new baby, giving hope for the future of the species.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: With JB and Lauren’s help, Tanya lets go of her parents’ treasured items to fund a dream trip to India, thus honouring her father’s memory and reconnecting with her heritage.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: After the scorching heat of early summer, Monty is working to keep the garden looking its best through the season's challenges. He tackles essential jobs, including cutting back perennials to make room for more colour, tending long grass and pruning step-over apples, while proving there's still time to sow vegetables for delicious late-summer harvests. Best of all, he enjoys some of summer's greatest rewards – lifting the first new potatoes and gathering fragrant sweet peas for the house. Adam Frost discovers a remarkable 2.5-acre walled garden in Surrey packed with hidden gems and unexpected delights. Sue Kent demonstrates how imagination and resourcefulness can transform everyday items into beautiful and productive growing spaces, while in Cornwall, there's a fruit and vegetable professor whose lifelong fascination with pests and diseases is helping gardeners grow healthier crops. Plus a visit to a grower with a striking collection of crassulas, showcasing the extraordinary beauty and diversity of these popular succulents.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Kemah Bob, JJ Chalmers, Kevin Clifton and Victoria Derbyshire take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games kitchen composter?

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp hosts this quiz show with sidekick Sarah Greene where contestants race on moving podiums across the studio floor. Correct answers get the players moving, while wrong answers stop them in their tracks. Five contestants battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - make it to the finish line before your rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the £5,000 cash prize.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Self-taught farming couple SJ and Nat rear their herd of free range pigs in the Pennines near Todmorden, West Yorkshire, and over the past decade have built up a farm that now supports them. They are proud of their award-winning sausages and hope their passion for high-welfare meat, and their freshly cooked samples, will attract plenty of attention at the Lancashire Game and Country Festival. As well as notching up sausage sales, SJ is keen to launch her new experimental products on the palates of passing shoppers. Meanwhile, for organic farmers Emma and Ian, who live in the Ribble Valley, it’s their first time at the festival. As well as aiming for a sell-out day for their grass-fed beef, it’s a chance for Ian to spread the word about their farm and reel in new trade. Emma focuses on her first love, the cows, and Ian is the people person. For both of these farming couples, the hard work of being at the festival will be worth it if they go home in an empty van and manage to win the hearts and loyalty of new customers.

programme.title: The Hidden World of Hospitality with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Tom visits three restaurants hoping to expand their businesses. Among them are a fusion restaurant combining Caribbean and west African dishes, a vegan restaurant and a pub-cum-butcher's shop.

programme.title: Wild Brazil
programme.desc: This intimate journey to the heart of a Brazil continues, following the animal families as they face the formidable monsoon rains. The giant otter babies must watch out for predatory jaguars and learn to swim before their river floods. The coatis must eat all they can before their world disappears underwater, and the youngest capuchin monkey has to learn some clever survival skills fast.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Norwich Cathedral in Norfolk, once home to Benedictine monks. Experts Thomas Plant and David Fletcher pick out some items to take to auction. Among them are an old astronomical map and a watercolour by a local artist. Meanwhile, Paul explores the history of the Broads.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, four previous players who came close to winning but never quite made it - Fern Britton, JB Gill, John Kearns and Sally Lindsay - take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. With their second chance at winning a prize, will they opt for the House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, four previous players who came close to winning but never quite made it - Fern Britton, JB Gill, John Kearns and Sally Lindsay - take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. With their second chance at winning a prize, will they opt for the House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Derbyshire with a recently married couple who are making an epic location shift and swapping the Blue Mountains in Australia for the wilds of the Peak District. They have a significant budget of £750,000 and have their hearts set on a character home with a large garden for their three dogs. Elsewhere, Jules comes across an unlikely centre of manufacturing nestled in the county’s rural countryside.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Digs in northern Britain reveal a Roman emperor’s lost bathhouse, the sunken treasures of medieval pilgrims and a formidable fortress perched on top of a Scottish mountain.

programme.title: The Devil in the Family: The Fall of Ruby Franke
programme.desc: Millions watch Ruby Franke’s wholesome YouTube content, but happy families are rarely as they seem. Could life coach Jodi Hildebrandt hold the answers to the family’s problems?

programme.title: Inside No. 9
programme.desc: Laurence has arranged a reunion with his old university chums Callum and Darren at a remote lake. It’s not quite what they were expecting, and Darren has even brought along his new girlfriend. But a lot of water has passed under the bridge since their college days, and as they strike out across the empty lake, the conversation takes them into choppier waters.

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: In the north east of England lies a wild and remote moorland. This ancient landscape of heather-clad uplands and sheltered dales is home to red grouse and tough moorland sheep.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Faisal Islam.

programme.title: The Inspection
programme.desc: A young gay black man, rejected by his mother and with few options for his future, decides to join the US Marines, doing whatever it takes to succeed in a system that would cast him aside. But even as he battles deep-seated prejudice and the gruelling routines of basic training, he finds unexpected camaraderie, strength and support in this new community, giving him a hard-earned sense of belonging that will shape his identity and forever change his life.

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Central Leeds crew, Nagina and Mo, are called to a scene where police are negotiating an end to a siege at which a man is threatening to harm himself and the officers attending.

programme.title: Evolution
programme.desc: Chris Packham embarks on an extraordinary journey through deep time to uncover how a creature as vast, complex and charismatic as the elephant came to be. From a tiny cell over four billion years ago to one of the planet’s most iconic giants, this is the surprising story of life's most radical transformation.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle celebrate 25 years together on Animal Park, looking back at some of the animals, keepers and unforgettable moments they have shared, while new stories continue to unfold across the park. It's a milestone moment for the park's young Amur tigers as sisters Ginger Biscuit and Seeka begin preparations to leave home. As part of the international breeding programme, they'll soon move to new collections to help secure the future of one of the world's rarest big cats. But before they can go, the keepers face the challenge of preparing them for life apart and training them to enter their transport crates for the very first time. As part of the anniversary celebrations, Ben and Kate look back at an extraordinary conservation adventure when Longleat keepers travelled to Tanzania to help renowned conservationist Tony Fitzjohn relocate endangered African wild dogs. This remarkable story would go on to influence conservation work back at the park for years to come. Elsewhere, Megan introduces the park's emus to an unusual new treat, while the recently arrived maned wolves begin settling into their new home as keepers learn to tell the three sisters apart.

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presides as members of the public with interesting items to sell come face to face with five dealers who bid against each other to try to bag themselves a bargain. Before facing the dealers, each seller has their item valued by auctioneer Simon Bower. Simon also gives them top tips on the history and key characteristics of their piece, so that when they enter the bidding room, they have all the information they need to try to haggle the best possible price from the dealers. All the dealers are spending their own money but have no idea what the sellers have brought in.

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: It’s fair to say that when it comes to classic national dishes, the French and British like to do things differently. However, both have a passion for poisson. Forty-five per cent of French households eat fish at least once a week, and Marcus wants to find out what kind of fish they like and if there’s a French dish to rival the great British fish and chips. If there's one fruit that might be even more synonymous with Provence than garlic, it's the olive. They’ve been grown here for over 3,500 years, and Marcus wants to understand the region’s passion for these tiny, versatile delights.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Political Thinking with Nick Robinson
programme.desc: Nick Robinson in conversation with people who influence our political thinking.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Kemah Bob, JJ Chalmers, Kevin Clifton and Victoria Derbyshire take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games kitchen composter?

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp hosts this quiz show with sidekick Sarah Greene where contestants race on moving podiums across the studio floor. Correct answers get the players moving, while wrong answers stop them in their tracks. Five contestants battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - make it to the finish line before your rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the £5,000 cash prize.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Debbie and Paul may not come from farming backgrounds, but Debbie’s determination after quitting her office job means they are now the proud owners of 300 alpacas on their Northumberland farm. They are heading to the Westmorland County Show in Cumbria, hoping to return home with rosettes and maybe even the champion breed title, which they have won before. Meanwhile, Lancashire alpaca farmer Bev also faces the heat of competition, and a champion win would take her one step closer to fulfilling her dream of full-time farming in the future.

programme.title: The Hidden World of Hospitality with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: The biggest celebrations in people's lives would be nothing without great hospitality operators working tirelessly behind the scenes. Tom looks at how the industry delivers exceptional service on a huge scale. In Wolverhampton, Tom meets the team behind Grand Station, a large events venue housed in a former railway station. In the Scottish village of Grandtully, the Highland Chocolatier supplies the trade with fine chocolates relied upon by the hospitality industry. And Tom has a huge catering event of his own: the Six Nations rugby match between England and France at Twickenham.

programme.title: Wild Brazil
programme.desc: This intimate journey into the heart of Brazil concludes. A fierce drought ensues, culminating in huge and ferocious fires. The capuchin monkeys, giant otters, coatis and jaguars are proving their extreme survival skills, while looking for mates and racing to breed to ensure that the next generation are born just as the good times arrive again. Astonishing footage tells an extraordinary tale of love in a harsh world.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Norwich Cathedral in Norfolk. In the great nave, experts Thomas Plant and David Fletcher pick out some choice items to take to auction. Among them are a masonic watch and a much sought-after commemorative mug by Eric Ravilious. Paul also visits the extravagant home of Britain's first prime minister, Robert Walpole.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, four previous players who came close to winning but never quite made it - Fern Britton, JB Gill, John Kearns and Sally Lindsay - take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. With their second chance at winning a prize, will they opt for the House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Edinburgh, 70-year-old Alister’s cycling accidents have resulted in a few knocks to his health and his confidence. Roman Kemp has a counterintuitive solution: easy exercise. It sounds backwards, but research shows that 'reverse' moves – like walking downhill instead of up, or lowering weights instead of lifting them – could deliver bigger gains in muscle strength, bone density and blood sugar control. Sometimes, the easy route really is the smart one.

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul follows in the footsteps of Bonnie Prince Charlie exploring the lesser-known lochs along the west coast of Scotland. Landing at Glenuig, he makes his way inland to Loch Moidart and MacDonald country, walking the old coffin road, which was used for centuries by funeral parties, taking their dead to a sacred isle. On the track Paul meets a trials bike enthusiast and learns the secrets of a 'dabbing coat' before landing off the Green Isle where he rings a 1,100-year-old bell for the dead. Taking a boat north, Paul sails up Loch Shiel to Glenfinnan where Bonnie Prince Charlie officially began his rising to reclaim the throne for the Stuart dynasty. From a forgotten monument that commemorates this event, Paul heads back to the coast and Loch Ailort, where he learns how to knife fight before taking a trip across the loch to a deserted community on the roadless Ardnish peninsula.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: It's all about the borders at Beechgrove Garden as Brian Cunningham and Kirsty Wilson replant, fill gaps and replace losses all around the plot. Kirsty revisits her hot border, designed to display vibrant colours at the height of summer. The winter temperatures killed off a few plants, so she will be trying out some alternatives. Brian is in the calendar border, created to have some form of colour or interest every month of the year. It has not had much attention this year, so Brian will be topping it up to ensure year-round performance. Kirsty will also be planting in the border behind Beechgrove's main pond, where other gaps have appeared, and she is building a new structure to create a habitat for pollinators. There is a trip to visit a great garden on the Cowal Peninsula, and all the week's handy hints.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: It’s the first ever RHS Sandringham Flower Show, and Arit Anderson, Jamie Butterworth and Frances Tophill are on hand to take a look around. From spectacular show gardens to a floral marquee packed full of horticultural goodies, there is something to whet the appetite. A local grower who knows everything there is to know about roses provides expert advice, while a National Collection holder in Lincolnshire shines a light on drought-tolerant achilleas. A couple in Kent share their new-found love of succulents, with some useful advice on how to care for them. And in Hampshire, a plantsman conveys his passion for carrying on the family tradition of running a nursery.

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: Bergerac is a broken man, battling his demons and on leave after the death of his wife. But when a member of the Wakefields, one of Jersey’s wealthiest families, is murdered, chief of police Uma Dalal wants their best detective on the case. The Wakefields, led by patriarch Arthur, are keeping their cards close to their chests, whilst a man from Bergerac’s past surfaces to make a shocking accusation. Is Bergerac up to the job?

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: The police are desperate to find kidnapped baby Lily before it is too late. Bergerac questions convicted kidnapper John Blakely, and the battle lines are drawn between these two old foes. But Bergerac doesn’t care who he upsets in his quest to find Lily and bring Cate’s killer to justice. Meanwhile, Bergerac disappoints his daughter Kim again and incurs the wrath of his mother-in-law, Charlie Hungerford. But he also uncovers the shocking truth of who kidnapped baby Lily.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Matt Chorley.

programme.title: Janet Planet
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Lacy is growing up in rural Massachusetts with her loving mother, Janet, in the summer of 1991. About to enter middle school in a few weeks' time, Lacy has a hard time making friends but has a healthy imagination, and she is captivated by her mother and the variety of the few friends her mother brings home.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Criminal gangs are targeting the rental market to take over properties and use them to run their operations. Panorama reporter Hazel Martin speaks to flat owners who paid reputable agencies to do tenancy checks but still ended up handing over their keys to criminals using fake IDs and references. One flat was turned into a drugs factory, another became a brothel, and a third was sublet and ransacked. Even when there appears to be clear evidence of criminal behaviour, gangs are escaping prosecution, and landlords say it can take months to repossess their properties.

programme.desc: Children's animation set on a remote rural farm. Shaun's flock lives a peaceful life under the watchful eye of their sheepdog Bitzer, but Shaun's insatiable curiosity often leads him into the unlikeliest adventures. A cabbage from a passing truck bounces into the field, and Shaun decides to use it as a football, but the pigs are out to spoil the sheep's game.

Trevor quests to solve the mystery, but having accidentally fallen into Whizzpop’s invisibility flour, how on earth will he get everyone’s attention?

programme.desc: When a witch struggles to master her new wand, Twinkle decides to have a go, only to make her friends vanish. She must now go on a solo quest to find them.

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Callum's pitting his Big Bro Adam against best mate Joe in this hilarious competition made up of funny, gross and gungy challenges. Who'll take the win and who will take a spin on the Wheel of Doom? It’s all up to Callum so they’ll need to impress him! When Joe and Adam open their front doors to find a 20m high crane outside, they know Little Bro Callum’s up to something! He’s challenged them to an epic Big Drop contest… with mum’s vase and their own phones. Which precious items will survive the drop, and how can watermelon and a gunge pool protect them? Once that’s sorted, it’s onto building gigantic hats with everyday objects in a bid to avoid Callum’s forfeit Wheel of Doom!

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Shaun steps in a can of red paint, and his resulting red hoof makes him a style icon on the farm. Before long, the rest of the flock try their own new looks and soon steal the limelight. As the outfits become more and more outrageous, Shaun wonders if less might sometimes be more.

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: Dennis and his mates rebel against an outbreak of unfair fun-killing rules that are being imposed on Beanotown.

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Callum is a Little Bro in control! His Big Bro Adam is going up against best mate Joe to see who will win a series of funny, gungy and gross challenges, and who’ll take on a forfeit from the Wheel of Doom. Callum isn’t taking any nonsense, so they have to work hard to impress him! The National Art Gallery has called: they need replicas making ASAP! It’s a job for Big Bro and Big Joe... using flour and gunge, natch. But whose art will be fit for a gallery, and whose will fail? Little Bro Callum decides the winner in this epic art showdown. Not all is lost, as the loser gets a chance to redeem themselves in the No Hands Challenge. Who can set up a Date Night using only their pockets, their wits and a giant shark with a violin? Sound extra? Callum’s challenges definitely are in this hilarious, gross and gungy contest!
